chief_complaint: Two patients, a 65-year-old male and a 70-year-old female, both with small cell lung cancer, presented with symptoms of diabetic ketoacidosis.
history: Both patients developed serplulimab-induced immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced diabetes mellitus (ICI-DM) after treatment with serplulimab. Case 1 developed delayed-onset ICI-DM after 2 years of treatment, while Case 2 developed ICI-DM earlier. Neither patient tested positive for diabetes-related autoantibodies.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia, tachypnea, and hyperglycemia. Physical examination revealed signs of diabetic ketoacidosis, including Kussmaul breathing and acetone breath.
labs: Hyperglycemia (blood glucose 350-400 mg/dL), elevated glycated hemoglobin, moderate C-peptide deficiency, and diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) confirmed by laboratory tests.
correct_diagnosis: Serplulimab-induced immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced diabetes mellitus (ICI-DM)
differentials: Serplulimab-induced immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced diabetes mellitus (ICI-DM)
Diabetic ketoacidosis due to other cause
correct_ranking: Serplulimab-induced immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced diabetes mellitus (ICI-DM)
Diabetic ketoacidosis due to other cause
